Multi-tenancy, machine-to-machine auth, and the attack protection suite require contacting sales
Additional dashboard users beyond the first three cost $20/user/month

faqs.answer: Yes, core authentication features are free at any scale when self-hosted, and the managed cloud service is free under 5,000 monthly active users.

faqs.answer: Yes, it's an open-source authentication platform with a self-hosted option.

features: All core features free at any scale
Pay only for premium add-ons if used

nuclei.name: Nuclei
nuclei.short_description: Nuclei is ProjectDiscovery's open-source, YAML-template-based vulnerability scanner for applications, infrastructure, and cloud platforms.

features.description: Uses a library of over 6,500 community-contributed templates to detect vulnerabilities.
features.title: YAML-based templates

features.description: Scans URLs, IP ranges, ASN ranges, and file-based inputs.
features.title: Flexible targeting

features.description: Runs scans in parallel to reduce time for large-scale assessments.
features.title: Parallel scanning

features.description: Supports TCP, DNS, HTTP, and file-based protocols.
features.title: Multi-protocol support

features.description: Handles HTTP basic auth, JWT tokens, and other authentication mechanisms.
features.title: Authentication support

features.description: Integrates into CI/CD pipelines for automated security testing.
features.title: CI/CD integration

features.description: Optional cloud features add reporting, CI/CD integration, and ticketing system integrations such as Jira and Splunk.
features.title: Cloud reporting and ticketing

pros: Free and open source CLI tool with a large community template library
Fast parallel scanning suited to large-scale assessments
Supports multiple protocols beyond HTTP, including DNS and TCP
Optional cloud tier adds reporting and ticketing integrations with Jira and Splunk
Actively maintained by ProjectDiscovery with frequent template contributions
cons: Advanced reporting and ticketing integrations require the paid cloud tier
Effectiveness depends on the quality and coverage of community templates
Command-line first tool that requires scripting knowledge to use effectively

faqs.answer: Yes, the core Nuclei CLI scanner is open source; ProjectDiscovery also offers optional paid cloud features.

faqs.answer: Templates are YAML files that define how Nuclei detects a specific vulnerability or misconfiguration.

faqs.answer: Yes, the cloud tier supports integrations with ticketing systems such as Jira and Splunk.

faqs.answer: Nuclei is maintained by ProjectDiscovery.

faqs.answer: Yes, Nuclei supports integration into CI/CD pipelines for automated scanning.

onedev.name: OneDev
onedev.short_description: OneDev is a self-hosted DevOps platform unifying Git, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, and packages, with a free Community Edition.

features.description: Combines Git, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, packages, and workspaces in one place.
features.title: Unified DevOps platform

features.description: Per-commit code search with language-aware symbol detection.
features.title: Code search and navigation

features.description: CI/CD as code with a GUI editor and multiple executor types, including containers, Kubernetes, agents, and bare metal.
features.title: Built-in CI/CD

features.description: Built-in registries supporting Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, and Helm.
features.title: Package registries

features.description: Task management with customizable issue workflows.
features.title: Automated Kanban boards

features.description: Email-to-issue linking for support and request handling.

features.description: AI integration described as supporting autonomous development work.
features.title: AI user automation

pros: Free Community Edition includes built-in CI/CD, package registries, code search, SSO/2FA, and LDAP/AD support
Combines Git hosting, issue tracking, CI/CD, and packages in a single self-hosted platform
Enterprise Edition adds HA/scaling, security scanning, and audit logging for $6/user/month
Includes AI user automation for autonomous development work
cons: Enterprise Edition requires a minimum order of 12 user-months
Security/compliance scanning, cross-project search, and HA clustering are Enterprise-only, not in the free Community Edition
Self-hosted only — no managed cloud offering found on the official site

faqs.answer: $6 per user/month, with a minimum order of 12 user-months.

harness.name: Harness
harness.short_description: Harness is a software delivery platform covering CI, CD/GitOps, feature flags, security testing, and cloud cost management, with a free tier.

features.description: Runs build and test pipelines as part of the broader Harness platform.
features.title: Continuous Integration

features.description: Automates deployment workflows with GitOps-based delivery.
features.title: Continuous Delivery & GitOps

features.description: Provides a developer portal module alongside the CI/CD pipeline tooling.
features.title: Internal Developer Portal

features.description: Feature flag management and experimentation tooling.
features.title: Feature Management & Experimentation

features.description: AI-assisted testing and site-reliability modules.
features.title: AI Test Automation and AI SRE

features.description: Cost optimization tooling for cloud and AI spend.
features.title: Cloud & AI cost management

features.description: Supports major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI) and other technologies.
features.title: 100+ integrations

pros: Free tier available for individual developers and small teams
Broad, modular platform spanning CI, CD, feature flags, security, and cost management
Open-source starter platform available (Harness Open Source)
Also owns and maintains Drone CI, giving it deep container-native CI heritage
cons: Paid Essentials and Enterprise tiers require contacting sales for pricing — no public pricing
Broad module set can be more complex than single-purpose CI tools
Detailed free-tier CI limits (concurrency, runners) are only shown at the Essentials level and above

faqs.answer: Yes, Harness offers a Free plan aimed at individual developers and small teams, alongside an open-source starter platform.
faqs.question: Does Harness have a free plan?

faqs.answer: The Essentials and Enterprise plans require contacting Harness sales; specific pricing is not published.
faqs.question: How much does Harness cost beyond the free tier?

faqs.answer: Modules include Continuous Integration, Continuous Delivery & GitOps, Internal Developer Portal, Infrastructure as Code Management, Feature Management & Experimentation, AI Test Automation, AI SRE, security testing, and cloud/AI cost management.

faqs.answer: Yes, Harness acquired Drone.io in 2020 and continues to develop Drone as 'Drone by Harness.'
